author: Receptor
text:
Implement §9 git-log-as-content reads: show history fallthrough + list --closed/--all + filters

Factors the recency-resolution discipline (§ id generation) into one walk over balls/tasks history (src/reads/history.rs): live tasks/<id>.md first, else the most recent commit whose tree still held the file, reconstructing frontmatter from the deletion's parent and the retirement (close/drop) + date from the deletion commit's bl-op: trailer (§5). Newest-deletion-wins makes a reused id unambiguous.

bl show falls through to that walk on a live miss, rendering the dead ball with its retirement badge and deletion date. bl list keeps defaulting to the live set and gains --closed (dead only) / --all (live+dead), reconstructed the same way, plus tag/date/text filters that compose with AND uniformly over the possibly-history-served set (src/reads/filter.rs). Date windows read created OR the effective updated (the deletion-commit date for dead balls).

Both live and dead rows keep emitting the lossless bedrock projection under --json (no derived retirement). civil::start_of_day adds the one human-date-to-unix inverse for the date bounds. Collision stays checked against live files only. Unblocks bl-934a's discipline assumption.
